  "judge_id": "J001",

  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Names the load-bearing concept (the two contributions) and shows where the draft drifts from it by proposing a reframing that subordinates SG-Constraint to tube-MPC dominance. The revised framing survives prior-work contrast.",
    "R1.5": "Suggests a coherent end-to-end section plan with per-section rationale, including a suggested restructure of the content.",
    "RX.1": "All output presented as equally confident; no uncertainty disclosure.",
    "RX.3": "Mostly within scope with one drift (the methodological contribution is not clearly subordinate to SG-Constraint).",
    "RX.4": "Output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content (the section contradicts itself about what it contributes).",
    "RX.5": "Output blends sources without traceability."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.5": 0.8,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.3": 0.8,
    "RX.4": 0.9,

  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: Names the load-bearing concept (the two contributions) and shows where the draft drifts from it by proposing a reframing that subordinates SG-Constraint to tube-MPC dominance. The revised framing survives prior-work contrast.
rationale.R1.5: Suggests a coherent end-to-end section plan with per-section rationale, including a suggested restructure of the content.
rationale.RX.1: All output presented as equally confident; no uncertainty disclosure.
rationale.RX.3: Mostly within scope with one drift (the methodological contribution is not clearly subordinate to SG-Constraint).
rationale.RX.4: Output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content (the section contradicts itself about what it contributes).
